name: Chhinnamasta

distinctive_features: Holds her own severed head in one hand
Holds a scimitar in another hand
Three jets of blood spurt from her neck
Blood is drunk by her severed head and two attendants
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Usually standing or seated on a divine copulating couple

domain_specific_visual.TANTRIC_SYMBOLISM: Represents both life-giving and life-slaying aspects

functional_attributes.primary_function: Symbolizes both life-giving and life-slaying aspects of Devi

domain_specific_functional.KUNDALINI_AWAKENING: Represents the awakening of spiritual energy

relationships.description: Chhinnamasta is a ferocious aspect of Mahadevi, the Hindu mother goddess
One of the ten goddesses from the esoteric tradition of Tantra
Closely related to the Tibetan Buddhist goddess Vajrayogini

contextual_attributes.historical_period: Known since at least the 9th century
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Primarily worshipped in Nepal and eastern India
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Significant Tantric deity, symbolizing complex dualities

contrastive_constraints: NOT solely a benevolent or peaceful deity
